A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The assistant computed returns using a month-end purchase rule in execute_python (grouping by month and taking the last CloseUSD), which is a planning choice that diverges from the ground truth’s methodology and led to a different top-5 list.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no

The agent computed DCA returns per index starting from each index’s own first available date ≥2000 (e.g., NSEI begins 2007-09 per var_call_ZUduLRJb6ZkTVEwaIgvofRYU) rather than enforcing a common start at 2000 for all indices, causing an apples-to-oranges comparison and yielding a different top 5 than the ground truth.

In the execute_python step, the agent computed each index’s performance up to its own last available date (groupby('Index').last()), rather than aligning all indices to a common end date, so indices with more recent data (e.g., through 2021) were favored over those ending earlier (e.g., 000001.SS through 2015), yielding a different top-5 than the ground truth.
